profile_backed_target.source: BlackGlen inbox on 2026-06-03 reported eval/nextafter_1k_f64 around 11.170 us after fj-lax I64 comparison work.
profile_backed_target.fresh_baseline_command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-nextafter-pass39-baseline-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/nextafter_1k_f64 --sample-size 30 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

optimization.lever: Same-shape DType::F64 tensor-tensor fast path in eval_nextafter.
optimization.guard: both operands are tensors; shapes are equal; both declared dtypes are F64; every element is Literal::F64Bits

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-nextafter-pass39-after-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/nextafter_1k_f64 --sample-size 30 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

benchmark.worker_caveat: Baseline and after are both remote RCH Criterion runs but landed on different workers. RCH exec does not expose a worker pin flag; the speedup is large enough to clear the keep threshold despite the cross-worker caveat.

isomorphism_proof.ordering_preserved: Yes. The fast path emits one output per pair in the same zipped row-major tensor order as the generic equal-shape broadcast loop.
isomorphism_proof.dtype_and_shape: Yes. The fast path returns DType::F64 with lhs.shape.clone(), matching the generic same-F64 tensor output dtype and equal-shape broadcast output shape.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Identical for the guarded case. It calls the existing next_after_f64 helper with the same f64::from_bits operands and stores results through Literal::from_f64, preserving NaN, signed zero, equal operand, and one-ULP behavior.
isomorphism_proof.malformed_tensor_fallback: Yes. Declared-F64 tensors containing any non-F64Bits literal return None and fall through to the generic per-element path.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: N/A. Nextafter has no ordering ties beyond x == y, which still returns y through the existing helper.
isomorphism_proof.rng: N/A. Nextafter is deterministic and consumes no random state.
isomorphism_proof.scope: Only equal-shape F64 tensor-tensor Nextafter changes. Scalar, tensor-scalar, scalar-tensor, F32, mixed dtype, broadcast, and shape-error paths are unchanged.
